name: AudioKit Music App Wisdom
description: Rules for generating macOS audio/music/MIDI apps with AudioKit. Captured from successful QAFlywheel runs against the audiokit-midi-synth scaffold.
appliesWhen: User prompt mentions audio, music, MIDI, synthesizer, drum machine, sequencer, DAW

rules.title: AudioKit Package.swift MUST contain ALL THREE scaffold dependencies
rules.applies_when: Generating Package.swift for any macOSApp that uses AudioKit
rules.guidance: The scaffold injected above has EXACTLY 3 .package() entries and 3 .product() entries. Your generated Package.swift MUST have the same 3 of each — do NOT drop any. The three packages provide DIFFERENT types, and the scaffold code references all of them. Type-to-package mapping: (a) AudioKit package → AudioEngine, Mixer, Settings, MIDINoteNumber, AmplitudeEnvelope. (b) AudioKitUI package → FFTView, NodeOutputView, and related visualization types. (c) SoundpipeAudioKit package → DynamicOscillator, Oscillator, CostelloReverb, Reverb, Delay, Distortion, FMOscillator, FMOscillatorBank, OscillatorBank, PluckedString, and most concrete DSP nodes. If your code uses ANY of those Soundpipe types (which the scaffold does), you MUST keep SoundpipeAudioKit in the dependencies — dropping it is the #1 reason AudioKit builds fail with 'cannot find type X in scope'. Versions: copy from: "5.6.0" / "0.3.0" / "5.6.0" byte-for-byte — DO NOT use 5.10.0 / 5.12.0 / 6.0.0 / ^5.0.0 (those don't exist). Platform: platforms: [.macOS(.v14)] — do NOT write .v12, .v13, .v15, or .v16. The scaffold code uses @Observable which requires macOS 14+.
rules.learnedFrom: 2026-04-13 AudioKit canary runs v4-v8: generator repeatedly (a) invented 5.10.0/5.12.0 versions that don't exist, (b) dropped AudioKitUI + SoundpipeAudioKit packages while using OscillatorBank/CostelloReverb/KeyboardView types that LIVE in those packages. Each auto-fix cycle re-produced the same missing-type errors because the LLM kept matching 'AudioKit = one package.' Needs explicit type-to-package mapping to break that pattern.

rules.title: Preserve the scaffold's exact Swift filenames and class names
rules.applies_when: Generating or auto-fixing an AudioKit macOS app based on the scaffold
rules.guidance: The scaffold ships with specific filenames (SynthConductor.swift, ContentView.swift, AudioKitMIDISynthApp.swift) and class names (SynthConductor, SimplePianoKeyboard). Do NOT rename these to 'AudioEngineManager.swift', 'SynthEngine.swift', 'AudioManager.swift' etc. — the scaffold's tests and the auto-fix re-injection both reference the original names. Renaming breaks subsequent auto-fix cycles because the reinjected scaffold no longer matches your output's file structure. Extend by ADDING files (e.g., 'SequencerGridView.swift' for a sequencer add-on), not by renaming existing ones.
rules.example: Keep: Sources/AudioKitMIDISynth/SynthConductor.swift (class SynthConductor). Add: Sources/AudioKitMIDISynth/SequencerGridView.swift (new file for a sequencer grid).
rules.learnedFrom: 2026-04-14 canary v12 where generator renamed SynthConductor.swift → AudioEngineManager.swift → SynthEngine.swift across 5 auto-fix cycles, each re-introducing compile errors because the scaffold reinjection kept bringing back the original names.

rules.title: Use Conductor pattern, not raw engine in views
rules.guidance: Wrap AudioEngine, oscillators, effects in an ObservableObject 'Conductor'. Views call high-level methods (noteOn, setReverbAmount). Never connect AudioKit nodes from inside View body.
rules.example: final class SynthConductor: ObservableObject { private let engine = AudioEngine(); ... }

rules.title: AudioEngine.start() on main thread, after view appears
rules.guidance: Call try engine.start() inside .onAppear, not in the conductor's init(). Calling from background thread or before the view is mounted causes silent failure on macOS 14+.

rules.title: Microphone entitlement required even for output-only apps
rules.applies_when: Any AudioKit app, even synths that never record
rules.guidance: AudioKit initializes the audio I/O subsystem, which requires com.apple.security.device.microphone entitlement. Without it, engine.start() throws on sandboxed apps. Use frameworks/_index/entitlements/audiokit.plist as the template.

rules.title: Map MIDI velocity (0-127) to amplitude (0.0-1.0) explicitly
rules.applies_when: Triggering notes from MIDI or keyboard
rules.guidance: DynamicOscillator.amplitude expects 0.0–1.0. MIDI velocity is 0–127. Always divide: oscillator.amplitude = AUValue(velocity) / 127.0. Don't pass raw velocity.

rules.title: AudioKitUI KeyboardView needs explicit frame height
rules.applies_when: Using KeyboardView or KeyboardControl in SwiftUI
rules.guidance: Without a height constraint the keyboard collapses to 0pt. Wrap with .frame(height: 120-160).

rules.title: Connect all nodes before engine.start()
rules.applies_when: Building effects chains or multi-track mixers
rules.guidance: AudioKit can't reconnect graph nodes while running. Connect oscillator → envelope → effects → mixer → engine.output before calling engine.start(). To swap nodes later, stop, reconnect, restart.

rules.title: AudioKit types need @available(macOS 12.0, *) or higher
rules.applies_when: Wrapping AudioKit types in custom classes
rules.guidance: Don't slap @available(macOS 26.0, *) on AudioKit-using types — keep at 12.0. Reserve 26.0 only for app-level SwiftUI types using the latest APIs.

_seedingPlan: Initial 7 rules from manual scaffold authoring. Additional rules get appended automatically by QAFlywheel runs against this scaffold and its derivatives (V2 synth, V3 MIDI controller, V4 sequencer, etc. — see AUDIOKIT_MUSIC_APP_ARCHITECTURE.md).
